headline: Digital Health Records System Covers Only 35% of Hospitals
context: Malaysia national digital health records system MyHEALTH covers only 35% of public hospitals as of February 2026.

cards.big: Digital health records cover only 35% of public hospitals in 2026
cards.sub: Four-year rollout has missed 2025 target of 80% hospital coverage

cards.big: 285 of 148 public hospitals connected but 97 still use paper records
cards.sub: System incompatibility between state and federal hospitals blocks integration

cards.big: RM1.2 billion spent on MyHEALTH but interoperability gaps remain
cards.sub: Private hospitals excluded from mandate covering only public sector

cards.big: Patient record access enables 42% faster diagnosis at connected hospitals
cards.sub: Emergency transfers between connected hospitals reduced duplicate testing 60%

cards.big: Health digitization tests whether medical records modernization reaches beyond early adopter hospitals
35% digital health record coverage. Electronic records save lives only when the entire system is connected.
